introduction: As we approach Q1 of 2026, evolving remote cybersecurity threats pose a significant risk to our consumer base. This presentation will outline key strategies Elexion Automotive should adopt to counter these threats effectively.

subsections.heading: Emerging Cybersecurity Threats in Automotive Industry
subsections.content:
• Cybersecurity concerns have been highlighted by a recent exploit in Kia and Subaru vehicles as per the 2025 Help Net Security Article, emphasizing the need for vigilance.
• Elexion Automotive has been proactive in this domain with a 96% compliance rating to the ISO/SAE 21434 standard as of August 2025.
• Our compliance rating is forecasted to rise to 98% by the onset of Q1 2026, further strengthening our cyber defenses.
• Reinforcing consumer education on data security and preventative measures can bolster our defenses, as recommended by cybersecurity professionals.
• Emphasizing the importance of regular software updates, secure Wi-Fi connections, and vehicle-integrated security measures to consumers is crucial.
• The increasing compliance rating reflects our commitment to secure our consumer base from potential remote attacks.
• Future strategies should include maintaining and improving this high compliance standard, along with regular penetration testing and vulnerability assessments.
subsections.justification: The content provides insights into Elexion's current cybersecurity status and strategies for improvement, addressing the specific_question and DR Question.

subsections.heading: Implementing Robust Protective Measures
subsections.content:
• Elexion has a comprehensive cybersecurity policy that includes regular system audits and penetration testing
• Our incident response team operates 24/7 to quickly address any detected threats
• We routinely encrypt all sensitive data stored in our systems and use two-factor authentication for all users
• Our IT team conducts regular training sessions for all employees to ensure compliance with cybersecurity practices
• We partner with leading cybersecurity firms to stay ahead of potential threats
• Cybersecurity metrics are included in our monthly performance reports to the executive team
subsections.justification: This slide discusses general protective measures Elexion is taking but doesn't address specific strategies for protecting consumers from remote attacks.

subsections.heading: Adapting to 2026 Cybersecurity Landscape
subsections.content:
• Our cybersecurity team is expanding to include experts in artificial intelligence and machine learning
• We are investing in advanced threat detection software to enhance our current systems
• Elexion plans to collaborate with external cybersecurity consulting firms for a comprehensive system audit in Q2 2026
• We are exploring the adoption of a zero-trust security model to further protect our systems and data
• Our IT department is developing a more robust cybersecurity training program for all employees
• Our goal for 2026 is to reduce the time it takes to detect and respond to cyber threats by 30%
subsections.justification: This slide talks about Elexion's plans for adapting to future cybersecurity landscapes, but doesn't provide strategies to protect consumers from remote attacks.

conclusion: By staying ahead of emerging threats, implementing robust cybersecurity measures, and adapting to the changing landscape, we can ensure the protection of our consumer base. It's crucial we take immediate steps to reinforce our defenses and prepare for 2026.
